Petition For Removal Of The Administrator, Lakisha R. Sykes, As Personal Rep
Off Calendar May-20-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauff for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA"). Ruben Sundeen for Lakisha Sykes. Nancy Rasch, Guardian Ad Litem for Darnisha Hughes. Ernest Der for Debra Dolch. Michele Hodgson appeared by telephone for Alex Borden.

Mr. Sundeen clarified that his Client wanted to stay on as Administrator, but that she would consent to Debra Dolch being appointed in the event that she were removed. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions as to the removal of Ms. Sykes, appointment of a Successor Administrator, and the level of authority/standing Accelerated Inheritance had to nominate someone or remove Ms. Sykes. Mr. Sundeen also reported that the property was being cleaned out and that they were getting it ready to be sold. All parties agreed to the Court releasing the SFPA as it does not appear that they will be appointed. The petition for removal of Lakish Sykes was granted. Ms. Rasch will prepare the order. Status on filing of Ms. Sykes' final account was set for July 13, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition to appoint Debra Dolch as Successor Administrator was selected/granted. Status on filing of the Petition For Final Distribution by Ms. Dolch was set for December 14, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. McDaniels.

Petition For Removal Of The Administrator, Lakisha R. Sykes, As Personal Rep
Off Calendar Apr-06-2009 Continued To May-18-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Sundeen for Lakisha Sykes. Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Nancy Rasch, Guardian ad Litem, for Daneisha Hughes. Alex R. Borden appeared in propria persona by Court Call.

Mr. Sundeen stated that the Homestead petition was withdrawn, that there was no objection to the appointment of a private professional fiduciary, and that the Court has the authority to remove his client; he wouldn't object. He also stated that he would not consent to Mr. Borden as set forth on the record. Mr. Borden stated that the only thing left was his own petition and that he was nominated. The Court stated that there was also an issue with what authority Accelerated Inheritance LLC had to nominate Mr. Borden. Ms. Rasch stated that she had some concerns with Ms. Sykes continuing as the administrator as it took her two years to file the Inventory, that someone needed to be appointed quickly, that the property was vacant, and that she had some concerns with the company Mr. Borden works with. Mr. Sundeen stated that he preferred someone local and noted that Mr. Borden is involved with a company who turns loans around quickly. The Court continued all matters to May 18, 2009 stating all matters could be heard at the same time; including any new petition filed by a private professional.
Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA

Petition For Removal Of The Administrator, Lakisha R. Sykes, As Personal Rep
Off Calendar Feb-09-2009 Continued To Apr-06-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Sundeen for and with Lakisha Sykes (Administrator). Alex Borden (Petitioner) in propria persona appeared by Court Call.

The Court stated that an objection had been filed and noted that there was a jurisdictional challenge. Mr. Borden stated that a verified objection was filed, that efforts were made to effect service, that two declarations of due diligence were filed, and that it was a non-issue with the appearance of the Administrator. Mr. Sundeen responded that he could have accepted service on his client's behalf, that his client did not get enough time to respond, and that Mr. Borden did not follow the proper substituted rule. Mr. Borden stated that it appears counsel is trying to buy more time. The Court stated that it wanted to make sure service is appropriate and noted the matter would not be going forward today. Mr. Sundeen stated that the petition was filed in November yet it took Mr. Borden two months to serve him. In addition, he informed the Court that they had filed a Petition to Homestead. The Court ordered that the Administrator must be properly served; noting that her counsel has agreed to accept service, that it was ordering a Guardian ad Litem appointed for the Minor, Darnisha, and that the petitioner is ordered to serve the Public Administrator and the Guardian ad Litem once he/she is appointed. In addition, the Court continued the two petitions on for today to April 6, 2009 (the same date set for the Petition to Homestead), and informed Mr. Sundeen that something needed to happen to move this case along since it had been open for twenty months. The Court instructed Mr. Borden to follow the Court's procedure if he wished to appear by Court Call on April 6, 2009. Mr. Sundeen stated that he would file a notice and acknowledgment of service.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. McDaniels.
